action.reasoning: Observation: 25-epoch training with residual MLP (hidden=1536, 5 blocks, AdamW, dropout=0.05) completed successfully (val MSE logged), but eval failed with CUDA OOM in predict.py (full test set ~1.4M samples loaded to GPU at once, ~30GB usage during forward). Prior best mean_r2 still ~-2.45e7 (ptend_q ~-5.67e7 dominant failure; t/rad/precip OK at 0.57/0.95/0.72). Analysis: Architecture change helped training stability but predict script lacks batching (unlike train/val DataLoaders); OOM root cause is large test tensor + intermediate activations in deep/wide net, not param count (~24M). Curves uninformative due to epoch-level logging only. Decision: Fix predict.py for batched inference (bs=8192, safe for 31GB GPU), submit_eval on existing checkpoints/best.pt immediately to obtain score for this model without retraining (saves time). Next steps will depend on whether ptend_q r2 improved.
